core_model.structure_summary: A generative latent-variable model on higher-order interactions in which each interaction's count is Poisson-distributed with a rate given by a bilinear form of per-node soft membership vectors and a symmetric block-affinity matrix, with node attributes drawn Bernoulli from membership-weighted probabilities.

variables.meaning: observed higher-order interaction counts and binary node attributes
per-node soft membership vectors, block-affinity matrix, and attribute-membership matrix
the log-likelihood being maximized over the latent parameters

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Maximization of a log-likelihood in which the mean interaction rate is a low-rank bilinear form of membership matrices and a block matrix, subject to a normalization constraint on memberships.

notes: Core is a mixed-membership stochastic block model for (attributed) hypergraphs: a Poisson likelihood with a bilinear u W u rate plus a Bernoulli attribute likelihood. The dimensionality-reduction/visualization layer (HyperNEO) is downstream and not the central generative model. Deterministic skeleton is the maximum-likelihood fit of the low-rank bilinear mean structure lambda = U W U^T.
